title: Eruptive Vietnamese Chicken Pho
description: A fiery, smoky pho featuring tender chicken breast, aromatic herbs, and a fragrant, low-FODMAP broth that dazzles guests.
instructions: Heat smoky olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add sliced ginger, celery, carrot, and scallion greens, sauté for 3 minutes.
Pour in the low-FODMAP chicken stock and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add chicken breasts and simmer, uncovered, for 15 minutes until cooked through.
Remove the chicken, shred it with a fork, and set aside.
In a separate pan, cook rice noodles according to package instructions, drain, and rinse with cold water.
Return the pot to the stove, add fish sauce and lime juice, adjust seasoning with salt if needed, and keep the broth warm.
Divide the noodles among bowls, top with shredded chicken and fresh herbs.
Ladle hot broth over each bowl, garnish with extra basil and cilantro.
Serve immediately, letting the broth steam and mingle with the smoky flavors.
